document.title: Digital chest radiography with a large-area flat-panel silicon X-ray detector: clinical comparison with conventional radiography
standards: Leitlinien der Bundesärztekammer zur Qualitätssicherung in der Röntgendiagnostik

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical comparison of a Canon CXDI-11 amorphous silicon flat-panel digital chest radiography system with conventional screen-film radiography.
sourceSummary.officialUse: May support clinical validation and verification literature for digital flat-panel X-ray detector image quality; it is not a regulatory approval document.
keyPoints: The study compared digital and conventional posteroanterior and lateral chest radiographs in 115 patients.
The digital system performed significantly better for PA overall appearance and most PA anatomic regions, except peripheral pulmonary vasculature and hilum.
For lateral images, overall appearance showed no significant difference; trachea, costodiaphragmatic recess, and hilum were rated worse, while retrosternal and retrocardiac lung were rated better.
The authors concluded the system showed statistically superior visualization for PA images and ambiguous performance for lateral images compared with conventional radiography.
The document states that the interface is DICOM 3.0 compatible.

entities.name: CXDI-11
entities.evidence: flat-panel X-ray detector based on a gadolinium scintillator layer and an amorphous silicon photoelectric converter/readout array (CXDI-11, Canon, Tokyo, Japan)

entities.name: Digital radiography system (DRS)
entities.evidence: A new digital chest radiography system (DRS) that uses a flat-panel X-ray detector

entities.name: Thoramat
entities.evidence: Conventional standard PA and lateral examinations were performed with a dedicated automatic chest system (Thoramat, Siemens, Erlangen, Germany)

entities.name: Institutional review board
entities.evidence: The study was ethically approved by an institutional review board.

concepts.evidence: This paper reports a clinical test of DRS for chest image quality

concepts.name: Large-area flat-panel silicon X-ray detector
concepts.evidence: Digital chest radiography with a large-area flat-panel silicon X-ray detector

concepts.name: Amorphous silicon thin-film transistor technology
concepts.evidence: flat-panel detectors based on amorphous silicon thin-film transistor technologies

concepts.name: Gadolinium scintillator layer
concepts.evidence: uses a flat-panel X-ray detector based on a gadolinium scintillator layer

concepts.name: Image processing parameters
concepts.evidence: Digital image processing was performed according to the technique available with the CXDI-II system.

concepts.name: Informed consent
concepts.evidence: Informed consent was obtained from each patient after the nature of the procedure had been fully explained

evidence.claim: The article was published in European Radiology in 2001.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital chest radiography with a large-area flat-panel silicon X-ray detector

evidence.claim: The document title is Digital chest radiography with a large-area flat-panel silicon X-ray detector: clinical comparison with conventional radiography.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital chest radiography with a large-area flat-panel silicon X-ray detector: clinical comparison with conventional radiography
evidence.pageTarget: Digital chest radiography with a large-area flat-panel silicon X-ray detector

evidence.claim: The source describes a radiologists' preference study comparing digital chest radiography with conventional radiography.
evidence.evidenceText: This was a radiologists' preference study to compare a digital chest radiography system that utilizes a large-area silicon flat-panel detector with conventional radiography

evidence.claim: The clinical study included 115 patients with both conventional and digital PA and lateral chest radiographs.
evidence.evidenceText: Conventional and digital posteroanterior (PA) and lateral chest radiographs were obtained in 115 patients.
evidence.pageTarget: Clinical validation of digital chest radiography

evidence.claim: The digital detector model used was CXDI-11 by Canon.
evidence.evidenceText: flat-panel X-ray detector based on a gadolinium scintillator layer and an amorphous silicon photoelectric converter/readout array (CXDI-11, Canon, Tokyo, Japan)

evidence.claim: The CXDI-11 sensor used 2688 by 2688 pixels with 160 micrometer pixel pitch and a 43 by 43 cm active area.
evidence.evidenceText: The sensor consists of 2688 x 2688 pixels with a pixel pitch of 160 mm. The active area is 43 x 43 cm

evidence.claim: The digital system output was 12 bits per pixel corresponding to 4096 gray levels.
evidence.evidenceText: The digital output is 12 bits per pixel, corresponding to 4096 gray levels.

evidence.claim: The maximum possible spatial resolution of the digital system was 3.1 lp/mm.
evidence.evidenceText: The maximum possible spatial resolution of the system is 3.1 lp/mm given by the pixel size of 160 mm.

evidence.claim: The study adjusted digital automatic exposure control to radiation doses comparable with the conventional unit.
evidence.evidenceText: The automatic exposure control of the digital chest system was adjusted to result in radiation doses comparable to the dose level of the conventional unit.

evidence.claim: The study obtained informed consent from each patient.
evidence.evidenceText: Informed consent was obtained from each patient after the nature of the procedure had been fully explained

evidence.claim: The study was approved by an institutional review board.
evidence.evidenceText: The study was ethically approved by an institutional review board.

evidence.claim: The digital radiography interface was DICOM 3.0 compatible.
For PA views, the digital system performed significantly better overall and for most anatomic regions, except peripheral pulmonary vasculature and hilum.
evidence.evidenceText: For the PA view, the digital system performed significantly better for the overall appearance and for all anatomic regions except for the peripheral pulmonary vasculature and hilum
evidence.pageTarget: Clinical performance of digital chest radiography

evidence.claim: For lateral digital images, trachea, costodiaphragmatic recess, and hilum were rated significantly worse.
evidence.evidenceText: For the lateral digital images, the regions trachea, costodiaphragmatic recess, and hilum were rated significantly worse.
evidence.pageTarget: Clinical performance of digital chest radiography

evidence.claim: For lateral digital images, retrosternal and retrocardiac lung regions were rated significantly better.
evidence.evidenceText: The regions retrosternal and retrocardiac lung were rated significantly better.
evidence.pageTarget: Clinical performance of digital chest radiography

evidence.claim: The authors concluded the system may be suitable for digitalization of chest radiography after changing some lateral image processing parameters.
evidence.evidenceText: After changing some image processing parameters for the lateral view, this system may be suitable for digitalization of chest radiography.
evidence.pageTarget: Clinical validation of digital chest radiography

reviewFlags.reason: The source is a clinical literature article, not an official regulatory approval or authority-issued document.
No regulatory jurisdiction or competent authority is identified in the source text.
No revision or version number is present for the article.
Some OCR artifacts are present in symbols and punctuation, including character substitutions around parentheses and units.
The source metadata productSignal is Unclassified, while the article discusses CXDI-11; applicability to the submitted product is not established by the source text.
